headline: What Air Products and Chemicals (APD)'s Clean Hydrogen Exit and NEOM Ammonia Pivot Means For Shareholders
summary: In late June 2026, Air Products and Chemicals decided not to proceed with its Louisiana Clean Energy Complex and several related clean hydrogen projects, expecting up to US$2.90 billion in pre-tax charges mainly from asset write-downs and contract terminations. At the same time, the company moved to finalize a global marketing and distribution agreement with Yara International for renewable ammonia from the NEOM Green Hydrogen Project, signaling a shift toward cleaner energy opportunities...

headline: APD Exits LCEC Project, Finalizes NEOM Green Hydrogen Deal With Yara
summary: APD is exiting the LCEC project and other clean energy plans, while advancing a Yara deal to market renewable ammonia worldwide.

raw.headline: APD Exits LCEC Project, Finalizes NEOM Green Hydrogen Deal With Yara

headline: Air Products and Chemicals (APD) Surges 8.0%: Is This an Indication of Further Gains?
summary: Air Products and Chemicals (APD) was a big mover last session on higher-than-average trading volume. The latest trend in earnings estimate revisions might help the stock continue moving higher in the near term.
